description: Project-specific critical bounds and checklist for code review. All AI agents MUST enforce these rules when reviewing PRs.

review_critical_bounds.condition: Attempting to log complete sender, tab, request objects or raw HTML content.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Reject PR. Require using IDs only (e.g., sender?.id, tab?.id), and wrapping URLs with sanitizeUrlForLogging() and API errors with sanitizeApiError().

review_critical_bounds.condition: Attempting to store large HTML content, Base64 images, or heavy payloads into chrome.storage.sync.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Reject PR. Require storing large payloads in chrome.storage.local or IndexedDB.

review_critical_bounds.condition: Introducing new core business logic without corresponding unit or E2E tests.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Reject PR. Require TDD and ensure Codecov checks do not fail (< 50%).

review_critical_bounds.condition: Classifying an issue as CRITICAL or security-related without citing the specific source line and providing a concrete reproduction scenario.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Require reviewer to re-verify against source code. Downgrade severity if evidence is insufficient or relies on assumptions about runtime behavior not confirmed by code.

review_critical_bounds.condition: Exposing internal-only, non-actionable, non-blocking cleanup/reconciliation/retry failures directly as user-facing errors.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Require keeping the canonical user-facing state, logging the internal failure, and handling retry/recovery inside the relevant service or canonical path unless the failure truly blocks the user's next action.

review_critical_bounds.condition: Treating low-risk, ephemeral, self-recovering internal state hygiene issues as must-fix findings when the proposed fix mainly adds control-flow branches, state coupling, or maintenance cost.
review_critical_bounds.enforcement: Reviewers must re-evaluate the real user impact, persistence scope, and recovery behavior, then preserve the downgrade evidence with the review decision, including source line references, a summary of the affected scope/impact, and the recovery or mitigation rationale. Downgrade the finding to a note or acceptable technical debt only if it causes no durable data corruption, user-facing inconsistency, blocked actions, or expanding side effects. CRITICAL or security-related findings must not be downgraded without citing specific source lines and a concrete reproduction or validation scenario.

quick_checklist: Single Responsibility: The PR focuses on one specific issue without sneaking in unrelated refactoring.
Log Sanitization: Verify all Logger.info/error calls correctly sanitize data (No PII, no raw HTML).
Data Isolation: Verify cache/large data usage strictly targets storage.local.
Language: Verify code comments and documentation are maintained in Traditional Chinese.
Evidence-Based Severity: Every CRITICAL or security-related finding must cite specific source lines and a concrete reproduction scenario. Downgrade if unverified.
Error Boundary: Verify internal-only, non-actionable, non-blocking failures are not surfaced directly to ordinary users.
Fix Value vs Complexity: Avoid escalating low-impact, self-recovering, non-persistent state issues into must-fix findings if the fix mainly adds branching, coupling, or maintenance cost.
